title: [Feature]: Add managed Kafka compatibility profiles
problem: Oracle GoldenGate lists Apache Kafka, Confluent Kafka, Amazon MSK, Google Cloud Managed Service for Apache Kafka, OCI Streaming with Apache Kafka, and Pub/Sub Kafka compatibility mode. nats-sinks has a Kafka sink backlog item, but it does not yet capture managed-service compatibility profiles.
proposal: Create compatibility profiles for future Kafka sink certification across Apache Kafka, Confluent Platform or Cloud, Amazon MSK, Google managed Kafka, OCI Streaming Kafka endpoints, and Pub/Sub Kafka compatibility mode. The profiles should define authentication, TLS, schema registry, idempotent producer settings, delivery confirmation, topic validation, and service-specific limitations.
users: Operators who want one Kafka sink contract with clear behavior across common managed Kafka services.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success requires producer delivery confirmation for every required record. The profile must document idempotent producer settings, retries, ordering, and duplicate risks for each service.
security: Broker endpoints, SASL credentials, client certificates, schema registry credentials, topics, and payloads must be redacted. TLS verification must be enabled by default.
acceptance: Research maps managed Kafka services to one Kafka sink certification matrix.
Authentication and TLS requirements are documented for each service.
Service-specific limits and unsupported features are captured.
Unit tests use fake producers and no network calls.
Documentation explains when a separate sink is not required.
tests: Add compatibility-profile tests around config validation and producer options once Kafka sink work starts. Gate live tests per service.
documentation: Update future Kafka sink docs and roadmap with managed-service profiles.
closeout: Close after the profile matrix is released.
